body:
## Summary

Adds a **Dark mode** toggle to the settings page.

- `settings.html` — new `Dark mode` checkbox (`#darkmode`) inside `#settings`.
- `src/styles.css` — dark-theme rules scoped under `[data-theme="dark"]`; light remains the default.
- `src/settings.js` — applies the persisted choice on load (reading `localStorage.darkmode`, comparing against the string `'true'`), toggles `data-theme` on `<html>` live on change. Persistence itself is handled by the existing generic `#settings input` change handler, so no duplicate storage logic was added.

Closes #1.

## ⚠️ Test plan — automated-verification decision is OPEN (merge-gate)

This repo has **no test harness** (no `package.json`, no CI, no test files). Per our automated-verification standard, I did **not** silently fall back to "manual verification" and did **not** silently install a harness (that would mean adding `package.json`/dependencies — a real cost the user owns). Both the planning and review comments on issue #1 escalated this as a decision for the user.

What was done instead, without mutating the repo:
- A **dependency-free logic sanity check** (plain Node, run outside the repo) confirmed the three behaviors that carry the bug risk: `localStorage` string-coercion (`"true"`), re-applying `data-theme="dark"` on reload, and clearing it on disable. All assertions passed.

**Before this PR merges, the user must choose:**
1. Stand up a minimal automated harness (e.g. Vitest + jsdom, or a Playwright DOM test that loads `settings.html`, toggles `#darkmode`, and asserts `localStorage.darkmode` + the `data-theme` attribute) and add the recommended test — or
2. Knowingly accept this single change on manual verification only.

This PR is intentionally **not** to be merged until that decision is made.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
title: feat(settings): add dark-mode toggle
